intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The expected specimens (P1-P4) are not visually identifiable as living botanical entities. The environment appears to be a collection of inert soil and debris.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only dark, damp substrate. No visible String of Nickels foliage.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot shows no evidence of Mexican Mint. Substrate appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot contains white debris (likely eggshell fragments) but no Pothos or rabbit anchor.
plant_audit.P4: No distinct specimen identified; shared space with P2 is devoid of plant matter.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots; no signs of active root respiration or growth.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, calcified fragments (eggshells) in P3. A metallic object (spoon) appears in T-3 and T-1, suggesting human intervention/maintenance attempts.
biome_observations.surface_conditions: No moss, fungal blooms, or secondary seedlings detected.

temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-3: Introduction of a metallic spoon in the yellow pot area.
temporal_deltas.t-3_to_t-2: Removal of the spoon; lighting conditions shift to lower visibility.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: Re-introduction of a white cylindrical object (possibly a sensor or marker) near the yellow pot.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Removal of the cylindrical marker; return to baseline state of total plant absence.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. There is zero evidence of photosynthesis or vegetative structure. The specimens have likely suffered total mortality due to environmental stressors or neglect.
anomalies: Intermittent appearance and disappearance of a metallic spoon.
Presence of white calcified debris (eggshells) in P3.
Temporary placement of a white cylindrical marker in T-1.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a failed botanical environment. Throughout the five-day observation window, no plant growth was detected. The pots serve only as containers for substrate and occasional non-biological debris. The 'rabbit' anchor is missing, and the 'String of Nickels' and 'Mexican Mint' are absent. The environment is effectively a dormant, non-productive soil system.
